scope_note: QA Steinmetz Polyphase Hysteresis cert [309]. Primary sources: Hardy, G.H. and Wright, E.M. (2008) An Introduction to the Theory of Numbers, Oxford, ISBN 978-0-19-921986-5; Wildberger, N.J. (2005) Divine Proportions: Rational Trigonometry, Wild Egg Books, ISBN 978-0-9757492-0-8; Steinmetz, C.P. (1892) On the law of hysteresis, Trans. Amer. Inst. Elect. Eng. 9:3-64 DOI 10.1109/T-AIEE.1892.5570437. CLAIM: (C1) Single-cycle orbit coupling sum S=sum_{k=0..23} cs(T^k(1,1)) is exact Fraction; cs(b,e)=(b*d-e*e)^2/((b*b+e*e)*(e*e+d*d)) in [0,1]cap Q; S=191484369324836748/23958756907457125 for seed (1,1). (C2) Maximum single-step coupling: max cs_k=1600/1681=(40/41)^2 at state (1,9) (k=22); G=G'=82 (symmetric case: both (1,9) and T(1,9)=(9,1) have quadrance 82); numerator=(1*1-81)^2=6400, denominator=82^2=6724. (C3) Polyphase coupling linearity: three 3-phase copies T^0=(1,1), T^8=(7,1), T^16=(4,1) each traverse the same orbit state-set (cert [303] triads); each sum=S; 3-phase total=3*S exact Fraction; parallel to cert [304] 3I theorem. (C4) Steinmetz exponent n approx 1.6 is observer; QA discrete cs values need no exponent parameterization; exponent approximates the cs_k variation at observer layer. (C5) Theorem NT: P_h=k_h*f*S_observer (float); k_h and B_max are material constants (observer); S is Fraction (QA layer); neither re-enters T-step. Builds on cert [303] (3-phase triads), cert [304] (polyphase orbit structure), cert [305] (cross-spread definition). Lineage: derived 2026-06-02.
